title: NOC 85101 – Harvesting labourers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 85101 represents the vital seasonal and year-round workforce that powers Canada’s multi-billion dollar fruit, vegetable, and specialty crop industries. As a TEER 5 occupation, harvesting labourers are the hands-on professionals responsible for picking, sorting, and packing the produce that feeds Canada and the world. In 2026, as the "Buy Canadian" movement and food security initiatives reach new heights, these workers are in record-high demand. For immigration, this role is a primary entry point through the Seasonal Agricultural Worker Program (SAWP) and various Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) semi-skilled streams.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category8 – Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupationsTEER Level5Major Group85 – Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourersSub-Major Group851 – Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourersMinor Group8510 – Agriculture, horticulture and harvesting labourersNOC Code85101

duties: Harvesting: Pick fruit, vegetables, and other crops by hand or using basic tools like shears and ladders.
Sorting & Packing: Grade and sort harvested crops based on size, quality, and color; pack produce into crates, baskets, or bags for transport.
Loading: Load and unload crates and containers onto trucks or trailers for delivery to processing plants or markets.
Field Maintenance: Assist in general field work such as weeding, thinning, and cleaning debris from harvesting areas.
Quality Control: Identify and discard diseased or damaged produce to ensure only high-quality items reach the supply chain.
employment_requirements: No specific education or training requirements are mandatory.
Short work demonstration and on-the-job training are typically provided.
Physical stamina and the ability to work in varied outdoor weather conditions (heat, rain, etc.) are essential.
Ability to perform repetitive tasks and lift moderately heavy crates (up to 20kg).
Basic communication skills in English or French for safety instructions.
salary_prospects: Note: Many harvesting roles also offer "piece-rate" pay (e.g., pay per bin or flat), which allows efficient workers to earn well above the median hourly wage.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. According to the Canadian Agricultural Human Resources Council (CAHRC), the sector faces a persistent labor gap that is expected to grow as Canada increases its fruit and vegetable exports. For 2026, the demand for mushroom and greenhouse vegetable pickers is particularly high due to the expansion of indoor farming facilities.
labour_market_demand: The demand for harvesting labourers is Very Good and highly seasonal, with peak requirements from late spring to late autumn. In 2026, Ontario (Niagara & Norfolk County), British Columbia (Okanagan Valley), and Quebec are the top regions for recruitment. With Canada expanding its greenhouse and indoor farming footprint, there is also a rising demand for year-round harvesting staff in climate-controlled environments.
High Accessibility: Minimal formal education or experience required, allowing you to start your Canadian work history quickly.
Modern Harvesting Tools: Gain experience with semi-automated picking aids, hydraulic platforms, and digital crop-tracking systems.
Outdoor & Active Lifestyle: Ideal for those who enjoy physical work in Canada’s most beautiful agricultural landscapes, from vineyards to orchards.
Bridge to PR: Many provinces offer dedicated pathways to Permanent Residency for agricultural workers who complete a specific number of work seasons.
pathways: Agri-Food Pilot (Successor Programs): Targeted at workers with experience in specific harvesting and food processing sub-sectors.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): Accessible for those working in the province's agricultural sector for at least 9 months.
Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): Targeted via the "In-Demand Skills" stream for workers in specific agricultural regions.
Saskatchewan Agriculture Talent Pathway: A specialized route for workers with a valid job offer in the province.
